title: Tell time to the nearest minute
description: Practice telling time using analog clocks. Some clocks do not have labels.
topic_path: Module 2: Place value and problem solving with units of measure
Topic A: Time measurement and problem solving
Tell time to the nearest minute

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the time on the following clock.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What time is it?**

The time is [[☃ numeric-input 1]] : [[☃ numeric-input 2]] a.m.
hints:
On a clock there are two hands. The $\redD{\text{hour hand}}$ is the shorter hand.  The $\blueD{\text{minute hand}}$ is the longer hand.



[[☃ image 1]]

The hour hand is between $1$ and $2$, so it is after $1$ o'clock but not yet $2$ o'clock.  



[[☃ image 1]]

So, the hour is $1$.

The minute hand starts pointing straight up for $0$ minutes.  

When counting minutes, the distance between each tick mark represents $1$ minute.


[[☃ image 1]]

Each number on the clock represents $5$ minutes, with a total of $\blueD{60}$ minutes in each hour.

We can relate the clock to a number line. 



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

The minute hand is at $32$ minutes past $1{:}00$.
The time is $1{:}32 \text{ a.m.}$

options.alt: A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every minute, showing the short hand between the 1 and the 2 and the long hand on the second tick mark after the 6.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

When counting minutes, the distance between each tick mark represents $1$ minute.



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

The minute hand is at $1$ minute past $2{:}00$.
The time is $2{:}01 \text{ p.m.}$

options.alt: A clock with every hour labeled and tick marks every 1 minute, showing the short hand between the 2 and the 3 and the long hand on the first tick mark after 12.

[[☃ image 1]]

The hour hand is very close to the $6$, but it won't be $6$ o'clock for $2$ more minutes. So the hour hand is after $5$ o'clock but not yet $6$ o'clock.  



[[☃ image 1]]

The minute hand starts pointing straight up for $0$ minutes.  

When counting minutes, the distance between each tick mark represents $1$ minute.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clock matches the time shown on the number line?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\goldD{A}$ is at $\redD8{:}{\blueD{28}}$ because it is located $\blueD{28}$ ticks from $\redD8{:}{00}$.
On a clock, $\redD8{:}{00} $ looks like this:



[[☃ image 1]]

And just like the number line in the question, $8{:}{\blueD{28}}$ is $\blueD{28}$ ticks from $8{:}{00}.$



[[☃ image 1]]

This clock shows $8{:}{28}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A number line labeled 8:00 to 9:00 with tick marks at every one-minute unit. The 8:28 tick mark is labeled A.

hints: The $\goldD{A}$ is at $\redD{10}{:}{\blueD{52}}$ because it is located $\blueD{52}$ ticks from $\redD{10}{:}{00}$.

On a clock, $\redD{10}{:}{00} $ looks like this:

[[☃ image 1]]

And just like the number line in the question, $10{:}{\blueD{52}}$ is $\blueD{52}$ ticks from $10{:}{00}.$

[[☃ image 1]]

This clock shows $10{:}{52}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A number line labeled 10:00 to 11:00 with tick marks at every minute unit. The 15-minute tick marks are labeled. The 10:52 mark is labeled A.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which clock shows $5{:}49$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
On a clock there are two hands. The $\redD{\text{hour hand}}$ is the shorter hand.  The $\blueD{\text{minute hand}}$ is the longer hand.



[[☃ image 1]]

So, $\redD5{:}{00} $ looks like this:



[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD5{:}{\blueD{49}}$ is $\blueD{49}$ ticks from $\redD5{:}{00}.$



[[☃ image 1]]

Notice the hour hand is past $5{:}00$ but not quite to $6{:}00$.
This clock shows $5{:}{49}$.



[[☃ image 1]]
